Title: AP2 transcription factor CBX1 with a specific function in symbiotic exchange in Lotus japonicus
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pht1 family phosphate transporter, mycorrhiza-inducible Pi transporter
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mediates proton-coupled phosphate uptake from the symbiotic interface into host root cells
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal uptake step at symbiotic interface
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential transporter for mycorrhizal phosphate uptake; key control point established in the paper context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential plant nutrient supporting growth and symbiotic nutrient exchange
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CBX1 activates LjPT4 expression; reduced LjPT4 transcript in cbx1 mutants and increased expression upon CBX1 overexpression support promotion of mycorrhizal phosphate uptake
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter mutagenesis; GUS reporter assay; EMSA; transactivation assay; cbx1 mutant analysis; overexpression in hairy roots; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mycorrhiza inducible in roots, specifically arbuscule-containing cells
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native LjPT4 promoter; mutated CTTC promoter; synthetic 4×CTTC-minimal 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving arbuscular mycorrhiza-mediated phosphorus uptake, especially by stacking with CBX1/HA1 modules under low-P environments
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Arbuscular mycorrhizal colonization by Rhizophagus irregularis; low Pi (100 µM); phosphate shift treatment (500 µM)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Dataset S2, from cited study); ChIP-seq (this study, no accession provided)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter activation by CBX1 depends on CTTC motif and cooperates with AW box; not fully explained by CTTC alone
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LjPT4 promoter activity in mycorrhizal roots requires the CTTC cis-element. 2. CBX1 directly binds the LjPT4 promoter and transactivates LjPT4 in a CTTC/AW-dependent manner. 3. LjPT4 transcript abundance decreases in cbx1 mutants and increases when CBX1 is overexpressed, linking CBX1 to symbiotic phosphate uptake.

Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane localized; periarbuscular membrane-associated symbiotic interface
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Proton motive force generation for symbiotic Pi transport
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes ATP to pump protons and energize proton-coupled phosphate uptake across the symbiotic interface
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies the electrochemical driving force required for target nutrient uptake
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream energizing step coupled to terminal phosphate transport
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for energizing mycorrhizal phosphate uptake
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential nutrient acquisition during AM symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Arbuscule-containing root cells at the periarbuscular membrane
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CBX1 coregulates LjHA1 with LjPT4, promoting the proton gradient needed for phosphate uptake; expression is reduced in cbx1 mutants and increased by CBX1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMSA on promoter region; qRT-PCR in cbx1 mutants and CBX1 overexpression roots; transactivation assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate partner for engineering improved symbiotic phosphorus uptake together with CBX1 and mycorrhiza-specific Pht1 transporters
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: R. irregularis colonization; absence or presence of AM fungi; low Pi
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Impicated in PAM functioning and nutrient uptake energization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LjHA1 is a mycorrhiza-inducible H+-ATPase gene coregulated with LjPT4 by CBX1. 2. CBX1 directly binds the LjHA1 promoter region containing the CTTC motif and activates its expression. 3. Reduced LjHA1 expression in cbx1 mutants supports its dependence on CBX1 for proper mycorrhizal phosphate uptake function.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fatty acid/lipid biosynthesis associated with arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates glycerol-3-phosphate to support acylglycerol biosynthesis required for arbuscule development and symbiotic lipid supply
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Contributes directly to synthesis of lipid products supporting symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key symbiotic lipid-biosynthetic gene implicated in arbuscule development
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports fungal colonization and bidirectional carbon exchange in AM symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CBX1 activates RAM2 expression, linking the regulator to host lipid biosynthesis required for mycorrhizal development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in cbx1 mutants and CBX1 overexpression roots; promoter transactivation assay; ChIP-seq-supported manual remapping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component for engineering improved AM symbiosis and carbon delivery to fungal partners, possibly stacked with CBX1 and phosphate uptake genes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq (manually recovered target due to incomplete genome assembly)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not initially recovered in stringent ChIP-seq target list because of incomplete genome sequence; manually supported as a CBX1 target
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RAM2 transcript abundance is reduced in cbx1 mutants and increased by CBX1 overexpression. 2. CBX1 activates the RAM2 promoter, indicating direct regulation of symbiotic lipid metabolism. 3. RAM2 links CBX1 to acylglycerol biosynthesis needed for arbuscule development.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2 family transcription factor; WRI1-like subfamily; double AP2 domains; C-terminal acidic transactivation region
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Mycorrhizal phosphate uptake and lipid/fatty acid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation through binding CTTC cis-element and AW box
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: LjPT4; LjHA1; RAM2; BCCP2; PDHC_E1α; PDHC_E1β; LPD1; ENR; GPDH; FatM; ABCB; Kelch; ACP1; MAT; PK; PPT; MIG1-like GRAS genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates LjPT4, LjHA1, RAM2 and multiple glycolysis/fatty acid biosynthesis genes; increases transcript accumulation upon overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: WRI5a; WRI5b; WRI5c mentioned as potentially redundant AP2 regulators; no direct physical interaction demonstrated
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Arbuscular mycorrhizal colonization; low phosphate favors mycorrhization; phosphate suppresses mycorrhization and associated transcription factors
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key AM-induced regulator coordinating bidirectional nutrient exchange by simultaneously promoting phosphate uptake genes and lipid biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant phosphorus nutrition and carbon/lipid supply to AM fungi during mutualism
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mycorrhizal roots, especially arbuscule-containing cortical cells
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CBX1 promotes mycorrhizal colonization and activates genes required for phosphate uptake and lipid biosynthesis; cbx1 mutants show reduced colonization and reduced marker gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMSA; promoter mutagenesis; stable GUS reporter analysis; cbx1 mutant phenotyping; overexpression in hairy roots, potato roots, and tobacco leaves; transactivation assays; subcellular localization; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mycorrhiza inducible; expressed specifically in root sectors colonized by AM fungus; correlated with LjPT4 expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Arbuscule-containing root sectors under AM colonization
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pUB promoter for ectopic expression; native CBX1 promoter for GUS expression analysis; 35S-based reporters in transactivation assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate regulator for engineering improved phosphorus-use efficiency and beneficial AM symbiosis by activating phosphate uptake and host lipid supply pathways, but likely should be deployed with tissue- or symbiosis-specific expression to avoid off-target effects
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizophagus irregularis or Gigaspora margarita inoculation; low Pi (100 µM); phosphate shift (500 µM); absence/presence of AM fungi
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ectopic expression increased triacylglycerols in tobacco leaves without changing total fatty acids; broader transcriptional effects on multiple AM-related genes may require context-specific deployment
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq (this study, no accession provided); RNA-seq integration with Dataset S2 from Vijayakumar et al. 2016
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CBX1 binds both CTTC and AW box motifs; both motifs cooperate in LjPT4 activation. Residual target gene induction in cbx1 mutants suggests redundancy with other AM-inducible WRI-like TFs, especially WRI5a/b/c.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBX1 encodes a mycorrhiza-induced nuclear AP2/WRI1-like transcription factor that specifically binds the CTTC cis-element and also the AW box. 2. CBX1 directly activates LjPT4, LjHA1, RAM2 and multiple lipid/glycolysis genes, coordinating phosphate uptake with lipid biosynthesis in AM symbiosis. 3. Loss of CBX1 reduces mycorrhizal colonization and marker gene expression, while ectopic expression enhances mycorrhization and induces target genes across eudicot hosts.
